center.name: 西巴布亚
center.summary: 西巴布亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联11种鸟类，包括白腹麻鸭、Snow Mountain quail、Mountain kingfisher等11种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The mountain kingfisher is endemic to the island of New Guinea, and is found in both Western New Guinea and Papua New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is monotypic within the genus Androphobus. It is found in Western New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is the only bird known to be endemic to the Bird's Neck in Western New Guinea, where it inhabits the Fakfak and Kumawa Mountains, two mountain ranges separated by 80 km of lowland rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The smoky robin (Melanodryas cryptoleuca) is a species of bird in the Australasian robin family Petroicidae endemic to West Papua, Indonesia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Snow Mountain quail (Synoicus monorthonyx), is a roughly 28-cm-long (11 in), dark brown quail of alpine grasslands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in remote montane forests of Foja Mountains range, Western New Guinea at an altitude over 1,150 metres

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The island whistler (Pachycephala phaionota) is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae native to the Moluccas and islands of Western New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The radjah shelduck inhabits the mangrove forests and coastline of New Guinea (West Papua and Papua New Guinea) and Australia, and some of the Moluccas in eastern Indonesia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The King of Saxony bird-of-paradise inhabits the montane forests of New Guinea, and is distributed from the Weyland Mountains in Western New Guinea to the Kratke Range and Mount Giluwe in Papua New Guinea between 1,300–2,850 meters above mean sea level, but usually between 1,800–2,500 meters above sea level

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Endemic to Indonesia, the western alpine mannikin is distributed in alpine grassland of the Snow Mountains and Star Mountains in western New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: mayri occurs in Irian Jaya, ranging from the northern Lake Sentani area and the Cyclops mountains across into western East Sepik in Papua New Guinea
